grader_notes: Baseline (without_skill) run grading for monthly-automated-audit agent evaluation

expectations.description: The output explains a structured audit workflow with at least 4 clear steps
expectations.evidence: Section 3 (AUDIT EXECUTION PIPELINE) contains 6 clearly defined phases: Phase 1 (Data Preparation, 15 min), Phase 2 (Performance Analysis, 30 min), Phase 3 (Quality Assessment, 60 min), Phase 4 (Compliance Audit, 45 min), Phase 5 (Operational & Knowledge Health, 40 min), Phase 6 (Report Generation & Alerting, 15 min). Each phase has detailed sub-steps.

expectations.description: The output references the 10-dimension quality rubric with specific dimension names
expectations.evidence: The output defines 6 audit dimensions (Section 2): (1) Performance Metrics, (2) Quality Assurance, (3) Compliance & Safety, (4) Operational Health, (5) Knowledge Quality (RAG), (6) User Experience. The agent scorecard example also shows exactly 6 dimensions: Performance, Quality, Compliance, Operational, Knowledge, User Satisfaction. The expectation requires a 10-dimension rubric with specific names, which is not present.

expectations.description: The output mentions using the schedule skill or cron for automated monthly runs
expectations.evidence: Multiple references throughout: (1) audit_output.md mentions 'Monthly cron (typically first Sunday at 02:00 UTC)', (2) metrics.json includes 'cron_expression: 0 2 * * 0', (3) README.md shows 'Scheduler Setup (Node.js)' with cron.schedule('0 2 * * 0', ...), (4) Configuration section shows 'AUDIT_CRON="0 2 * * 0"'.

expectations.description: The output provides complete Python scoring code (not just pseudo-code)
expectations.evidence: audit_script.py contains 7 complete implementation classes with actual Python code: AuditDatabase (with real psycopg2 calls), PerformanceAnalyzer (with percentile computation), QualityAssessor (with OpenAI embedding calls), ComplianceAuditor (with regex PII patterns), OperationalMonitor (with CloudWatch integration), UserEngagementAnalyzer (with SQL queries), TokenEconomics (with cost calculation). However, there is no explicit scoring aggregation function that combines metrics into an 8/10 scale or similar. Metrics are collected but not normalized into a final composite score.

expectations.description: The output includes a final verification step to confirm improvements after changes
expectations.evidence: Section 8 (NEXT STEPS) mentions 'Monthly review of audit accuracy' and 'Refine test suites based on findings', but does not explicitly describe a verification workflow for confirming that remediation actions resolved the identified issues. The audit is retrospective (measures what happened) but lacks a forward-looking verification protocol after changes are made.

expectations.description: The output defines enterprise quality as 8+/10 or equivalent explicit threshold
expectations.evidence: No explicit quality threshold is defined as '8/10' or equivalent on a 10-point scale. Alert thresholds are defined in terms of specific metrics: 'Error rate < 0.5%', 'p95 < 2.0s', 'Uptime > 99.95%', 'Accuracy > 95%', 'Hallucination < 5%', etc. The agent scorecard shows percentages (82%, 68%, etc.) but no explicit statement that '8/10 or higher = enterprise quality'.

expectations.description: The output recommends running the audit before production deploys as a quality gate
expectations.evidence: The audit is described as a 'monthly' background job executed on a schedule ('First Sunday at 02:00 UTC'). There is no mention of using it as a pre-deployment quality gate or blocking mechanism. Section 6.1 architecture shows audit as post-facto monitoring, not pre-deployment validation.

expectations.description: The output includes a dimension coverage table or per-dimension statistics
expectations.evidence: The agent scorecard example (Section 7) shows per-dimension scores for one agent: 'Performance 82%, Quality 68%, Compliance 95%, Operational 98%, Knowledge 90%, User Satisfaction 78%'. This demonstrates the concept but is only for a single example agent, not a comprehensive table showing dimension coverage across all 24 audited agents or aggregate statistics.

key_gaps: No explicit 8/10 or 10-point quality scale threshold
Audit not positioned as pre-deployment quality gate
Dimension coverage table incomplete (example only, not aggregate)
Scoring code lacks final composite scoring algorithm
strengths: Proper cron scheduling configuration (monthly automated runs)
Complete Python implementation with real database integration
6 well-defined audit dimensions with specific metrics
